calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-01-2022 Off Calendar

Declaration In Lieu Of Testimony And Statement Of The Case In Support Of Request For Court Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-22-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Prove-Up Hearing as to defaulted defendant Cade Peterson.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:50 a.m.

Lukas Sosnicki, Esq. and Alexander Carnevale, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Higher Education Loan Authority of the State of Missouri.
Julie Mauzy, Litigation Account Manager for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
Ms. Mauzy, along with Messrs. Sosnicki & Carnevale, appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Cade Peterson.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract, Unjust Enrichment and Declaratory Relief was filed on January 28, 2021.
Substitute service of plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendant Cade Peterson occurred on June 17, 2021, and was effectuated on June 27, 2021.
Default as to defendant Cade Peterson was entered on August 5, 2021.
No DOE defendants were named in plaintiff's complaint.

Plaintiff Higher Education Loan Authority of the State of Missouri submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Loan Agreement.............................................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 2: Bill of Sale for Loan Agreement............................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 3: "Mohela" Payoff Calculation Totals.........................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 4: Payment History under Loan Agreement.......................................ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Higher Education Loan Authority of the State of Missouri, and against the defaulted defendant Cade Peterson, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $49,094.09.
Prejudgment Interest - $2,202.99.
Attorney Fees - $1,050.00.
Costs - $1,614.50.

Total - $53,961.58.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Proceedings concluded at 12:10 p.m.
